channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: The Witcher 3 minimum PC requirements rise significantly ahead of new expansion, forcing you to use Windows 11 and an SSD
item.content:encoded:
An 11 year-old game renews its demands.

CD Projekt Red has updated the minimum system requirements for The Witcher: Wild Hunt on PC, most notably ending support for hard-disk drives and Windows 10. Only solid-state drives and Windows 11 will be supported going forward.

The move has been made in preparation for a third Witcher 3 expansion - Songs of the Past - arriving next year. CD Projekt Red said: "With new content coming to the game, we need to update our system requirements to ensure smooth performance and compatibility going forward for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt and Songs of the Past."

Starting from the next update, which is as yet undated, the minimum system requirements of The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, an 11 year-old game, will rise to:

  • CPU: AMD Ryzen 5 2600, Intel Core i5-8400
  • GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660, AMD Radeon RX 5500 XT 8GB
  • VRAM: 6 GB
  • RAM: 12 GB
  • Storage: 70 GB SSD
  • OS: 64-bit Windows 11

For comparison, the current minimum system requirements for The Witcher 3, as listed on Steam, are:

  • CPU: Intel CPU Core i5-2500K 3.3GHz / AMD A10-5800K APU (3.8GHz)
  • GPU: NVIDIA GPU GeForce GTX 660 / AMD GPU Radeon HD 7870
  • RAM: 6 GB
  • Storage: 50 GB available space
  • OS *: 64-bit Windows 7, 64-bit Windows 8 (8.1)

It's a considerable change. "These minimum requirements reflect how hardware capabilities and software usage have evolved since we last changed the system requirements," CD Projekt Red explained.

The switch to Windows 11 reflects Microsoft ending support for Windows 10 last year. "Without ongoing security updates, official platform support, and continued GPU driver support, we will no longer test our games on Windows 10," CD Projekt Red stated.

The Windows 11 requirement also affects other PC components in that The Witcher 3 will "exclusively" run on DirectX 12, and "only processors supported on Windows 11 will be supported by us", and "only graphics cards with ongoing active driver support for gaming on Windows 11 will be supported".

Hard-disk drives are no longer being supported because solid-state drives offer better overall performance and faster loading times and asset streaming, CD Projekt Red added.

Note, however, that you can revert to earlier versions of The Witcher 3 should you be potentially affected by this. CD Projekt Red has a guide on how to do this.

It's a curious change to make. On the one hand, it suggests there's an exciting level of technical ambition being employed on the third Witcher 3 expansion, and that CD Projekt Red and Fool's Theory - the co-developer - intend to make something that looks impressive by today's standards.

On the other hand, an 11 year-old game can't help but have a foot in the past, and there's a danger of alienation if people's PC hardware isn't powerful enough, or if people have made deliberate decisions not to move to Windows 10. It's not a forgiving market to upgrade your PC in, currently, either, with component pricing pushed sky-high by rampant big-tech demand for AI data-centers.

Nevertheless, it is impressive to see CD Projekt Red continue to expand The Witcher 3 so late in the game's life, even if there's a slight financial pressure here to hit revenue targets in order to meet company bonus targets.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/the-witcher-3-minimum-pc-requirements-change
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Another GTA 5 multiplayer mod service is shutting down as Take-Two and Rockstar push to consolidate modding around FiveM
item.content:encoded:
"RAGE MP will begin a structured shutdown process."

As we eagerly await GTA 6's marketing push to kick off, Grand Theft Auto 5 and GTA Online continue to make millions of dollars each month, so it's not surprising to see Take-Two push to consolidate multiplayer mods around FiveM, the platform it acquired back in 2023.

The latest blow to GTA 5's vibrant modding community comes through the announcement of RAGE:MP shutting down operations as soon as 1st June, 2026: "In accordance with that policy, and at Take-Two's request, RAGE:MP will begin a structured shutdown process. We are asking all server owners to wind down their operations and migrate to FiveM."

It's a bit sudden, but not entirely unpredictable. Take-Two and Rockstar have been quite vocal in the past about wanting to get all of GTA 5 and Online's modding under one roof, with Rockstar-approved mod tools being rolled out too.

Players outside the "public server listing" will have a bit more time to sort out and migrate their community servers though: "All remaining community servers are expected to cease operations by August 31, 2026. By that time, community servers must have migrated." RAGE:MP's game, server toolkit, and backend infrastructure will no longer be available after that date.

"We know this is tough news for everyone - both developers and players. As FiveM is now Rockstar Games' official platform for modding-based experiences, we've done our best to give you extra time to move your servers to their new home, and we will continue to do everything we can to make the switch over as smooth as possible," the official post by the RAGE:MP team added before thanking everyone for the time and effort put into the platform over the years.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/gta-5-rage-mp-mod-service-shutting-down
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon's Season 2 cinematic trailer promises things are about to get spookier on Tau Ceti IV: "Moon's EXTRA haunted"
item.content:encoded:
Marathon continues to grow, even as Destiny 2 is on its way out. Following an official announcement of Season 2 earlier this month, the cinematic trailer is promising the extraction shooter will get even scarier this summer.

Season 2: Nightfall was always meant to be a meaner chapter for the live-service game, yet recent updates - responding to player feedback - have confirmed PvE and "PvP-lite" modes have been locked in and are coming soon, too.

The trailer itself is all about spooky vibes, which is kind-of funny considering summer (and not October) is about to start, but hey, this also feels like a natural progression for a game that's already a bit scary to play. The night has indeed fallen on Tau Ceti IV, which means enemy encounters are tenser and something darker is rising... Are those multi-limbed, reanimated Runner corpses? We might be cooked, chat.

While we're still waiting for the full list of specific additions and changes coming with the 2nd June update, players are preparing for a brutal reset and infils into far more ruthless maps: "I spit my drinkable cheeseburger watching this," one user in the trailer's comment section said. "Moon's EXTRA haunted," added another one with a well-selected meme.

Despite Marathon's total numbers not being where Sony and Bungie probably expected them to be after launch, it's abundantly clear a hardcore community is emerging around the demanding online FPS. Its future - and Bungie's - depends on attracting and retaining a casual crowd too. Therefore, expect Season 2 to be a key one as the powers that be try to determine a satisfying path forward.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/marathon-season-2-cinematic-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The rumoured third Witcher 3 expansion has been announced by CD Projekt Red
item.content:encoded:
CD Projekt Red has announced the much-rumoured third expansion for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt. It's called Songs of the Past, it's based around Geralt again, and it's coming out next year on PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series S/X.

CDPR is co-developing the expansion with Fool's Theory, as has been rumoured. Fool's Theory is the studio made up of CDPR veterans that's remaking The Witcher 1 and helping on The Witcher 4. Fool's Theory also made the Polish-set role-playing game The Thaumaturge, which I thought was pretty good.

Songs of the Past is described in a press release as a "third expansion" for The Witcher 3, suggesting it's comparable in size to previous expansions Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine, which were sizable releases. Blood and Wine in particular featured a whole new region and nearly a single-player game's worth of adventure to have.

There's no detail on the content of the expansion other than that it will star Geralt - there's no mention of The Witcher 4 lead character Ciri anywhere. It being called Songs of the Past, however, does suggest we could be going back in time to before The Witcher 3 began, or perhaps to that period of time when Ciri was a child? We could even be going back in time further than that - to before CDPR's Witcher saga began.

More information is apparently coming later this summer.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/rumoured-witcher-3-third-expansion-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: First Metal Gear Solid 4 port footage confirms Konami is keeping those real world iPod and Playboy cameos
item.content:encoded:
Product placement will remain alive and well in Konami's upcoming and long-awaited modern port of Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots.

Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots will be included in the company's Metal Gear Solid Master Collection Vol. 2, which was revealed during the Sony State of Play earlier this year. This collection will also comprise Metal Gear Ghost Babel and Metal Gear Solid: Peace Walker.

Earlier this week, Japanese writer Mafia Kajita along with voice actor Yuichi Nakamura made their way through the first act of the Master Collection Vol. 2's version of Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots, giving us all a quick look at the game's iPod and Playboy collaboration cameos.

In Metal Gear Solid 4, Snake was able to listen to songs and podcasts on his iPod throughout the game. Evidently, Apple is keen to keep this collaboration going, even if Mp3 players like the iPod have significantly waned in popularity over recent years. What is less clear, though, is what tracks will be included on the port's release this summer.

During the same playthrough, we also got to see (very briefly) Snake scooping up a Playboy magazine. In the original game, these magazines could be found and then used to distract enemies.

It is unclear right now if other real world product placements, such as American soft drink Mountain Dew, will also be making a return in Metal Gear Solid Master Collection Vol. 2.

Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots was originally released for the PlayStation 3 back in June 2008, and earlier this year eagle-eyed players noticed that it had apparently been delisted from the PS3 store. The game will become available for modern consoles complete with a boost in frame rate and visuals when Metal Gear Solid Master Collection Vol. 2 releases on 27th August across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC (Steam).

In 2023, Konami released Master Collection Vol. 1, which included the first three Metal Gear Solid games plus some extra classics. However, there were some teething issues on its release, with Digital Foundry calling it "anything but masterful". This was all due to a "litany of issues", the team said in its review. A number of patches were then released to improve the overall experience.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/metal-gear-solid-4-playboy-ipod
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
